ontology.foodex2_description: Mooncake — baked pastry filled with lotus seed paste and salted egg yolk
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for mooncake; food-level coding not required in clinical SNOMED scope.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is half a standard baked mooncake, approximately 90 g, as mooncakes are traditionally shared and cut into quarters or halves.

nutrient_source.title: Baked mooncake (with yolk) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for 'Baked mooncake (with yolk)', CR ID F07030003. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Mooncake, lotus paste with egg yolk — GI analogous estimate based on 'Pastry, sweet, filled with lotus paste' and similar baked Chinese pastries
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for baked mooncake with salted egg yolk exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 61 is estimated from the closest analogous entry for sweet baked Chinese pastries with lotus paste filling, which fall in the medium GI range (55–65). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (61) × available carbohydrate per serving (44.24 g/100g × 90 g serving = 39.8 g) / 100 = 24.3 g. This is classified as high GL (≥20).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (30.9 g/100g) and moderate sodium (149 mg/100g) with low dietary fibre (2.07 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit baked mooncake with egg yolk to very small occasional portions (≤30 g) due to high sugar content driving insulin-mediated sodium retention, and the additional sodium contributed by the salted egg yolk; regular consumption is not recommended per AHA 2018 dietary guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (30.9 g/100g) and high carbohydrate (44.24 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (2.07 g/100g) producing a high glycemic load of 24.3 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Baked mooncake with salted egg yolk is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; sugar at 30.9 g/100g far exceeds the 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling, and the high glycemic load of 24.3 per 90 g serving will cause a significant postprandial glucose spike; if consumed at all, limit to ≤30 g with careful blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (23.85 g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g from salted egg yolk and vegetable shortening, combined with high sugar (30.9 g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of baked mooncake with egg yolk; the high saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g) from the egg yolk and pastry fat raises LDL cholesterol, and the high sugar load promotes triglyceride synthesis, both of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors per ACC/AHA 2019 guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar at 30.9 g/100g and estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g both exceed RA dietary thresholds
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption; high refined sugar and saturated fat promote systemic inflammation by upregulating TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines, worsening RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked mooncake with salted egg yolk (蛋黄月饼, Dàn huáng yuèbǐng) is a traditional Chinese baked pastry most closely associated with the Mid-Autumn Festival, widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, Hong Kong, and across the Chinese diaspora. The pastry shell is made from wheat flour, golden syrup, lye water, and vegetable oil, producing a soft, slightly chewy, golden-brown crust with a characteristic sheen. The filling consists of sweetened lotus seed paste surrounding one or more whole salted duck egg yolks, which provide a rich, savory-sweet contrast to the sweet paste. The mooncake is baked until the crust is set and the surface is golden. Per 100 g, it provides 419 kcal, 6.81 g protein, 23.85 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g from the egg yolk and pastry fat), 44.24 g carbohydrate, and 30.9 g sugar, with only 2.07 g dietary fibre and 149 mg sodium. The high sugar and fat content make it a high-energy, low-fibre food with a medium glycemic index (~61) but a high glycemic load (~24 per 90 g serving). Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its very high sugar, saturated fat, and moderate sodium content. It is a seasonal festive food and should be consumed in small portions on special occasions only.

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (CR ID F07030003) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at ~9.5 g/100g based on known composition of salted egg yolk and lotus paste mooncake pastry, consistent with USDA FoodData Central analogous entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar baked Chinese sweet pastries.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat), micronutrient panel (vitamins A, B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, C, D, E, folate), mineral panel (potassium, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium), cholesterol, and moisture data for this specific baked mooncake with salted egg yolk preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry F07030003, and no sufficiently close substitute entry covering all these fields is available in USDA FoodData Central for this composite traditional pastry as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat of 9.5 g/100g is estimated from known composition of lotus paste mooncake with salted egg yolk based on USDA FoodData Central analogous entries and is consistent with the input health_suitability notes. The input record incorrectly included 'plant_based' as a dietary tag; this has been removed as the food contains salted duck egg yolk and is not plant-based. Halal status is marked false as traditional salted duck egg yolk mooncakes may not carry halal certification; consumers should verify with the specific manufacturer. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this exact product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16.
